content.text: Near misses are itemized. So are miracles.
Attacks resolve in three bands like everything else: hit, graze, miss. And the spike has one trigger everywhere: natural maximum on your accuracy dice.
Graze: an attack total within 2 below the target's Evasion is a graze. Halve the punch-through (round down), then resolve normally. No crit effects. No on-hit riders.
Run the bounce gate and Value subtraction first, halve the resulting punch-through, then route it as usual (see Attacks & the Shell Clock).
Riders never graze-fish: if an effect needs a hit to trigger, a graze doesn't feed it. The only exceptions are priced augments on tree pages.

Crit: a natural maximum on your accuracy dice is a crit. The attack auto-hits — the total doesn't matter, the natural max is the hit — and the weapon's crit effects fire. Every weapon prints its own crit payout (see Weapons).

headers: Natural max on your accuracy dice means
rows: Auto-hit + the weapon's crit effects (see Weapons).
Flash of brilliance — the result upgrades one band (see The Two Rolls).

content.text: Same trigger, same words, two tables. The flash's own fine print lives on The Two Rolls.
Per actuarial guidance, "it only grazed me" has been reclassified from reassurance to data point.
